votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking that the lengths are equal and each element in the output is exactly one greater than the corresponding element in the input.

The Python code correctly checks the same conditions as the Coq spec: (1) length equality between input and output, and (2) for all valid indices i, output[i] equals input[i] + 1. The iterative loop in Python is equivalent to the universal quantifier in Coq for concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly checks the same conditions as the Coq spec: (1) length equality between input and output, and (2) for all valid indices i, output[i] equals input[i] + 1. The iterative loop in Python checking all indices is equivalent to the universal quantifier in Coq over the range [0, length(output)).
